reviews.author: Nguyễn Thị Thu Nga
reviews.text: Overall 4/5 Trekking Langbiang is rewarding experience with fresh mountain air, peaceful pine forests, and beautiful panoramic views over Dalat. The trail is easy to moderate, making it suitable for beginners, though it can be slippery after rain. Overall, it’s a great choice if you want a more authentic and less touristy way to enjoy Langbiang.

reviews.author: Dez
reviews.text: Entrance tickets was 100K for 2 pax. We took the unofficial unpaved roads that was next to the entrance area. Though we won’t in optimal hiking attire, we really wanted to try. We first walked the road till there was a fork and we took the left path which was characterised with clay and sand. 2 days ago it rained so the paths were abit muddy, had to thread carefully. After 20mins the path led to the entrance of the forested area, which looked like something out of a fairytale! We entered the forested area which was filled with pine trees and acorns peppered the ground. Reminds me of the scene in twilight where Edward jumped from tree to tree 😂 anyway we hiked abit in the forest and eventually came to a point where the ascent was very steep. Since we were not in proper gear, we were worried for our lives 😂 we crouched low to the ground and used our hands for more leverage. Thankfully we emerged from the mountain alive. It was a very steep climb and a few times we were out of breath. It was certainly difficult to navigate steep ascents without proper pathing and slightly wet ground! Oh, also there was this small Black doggo that hung around for abit and waited for us when we took a break. We thought it belonged to another young Vietnamese couple that was also on the trail but then I saw someone on Google posted a photo of that dog too! I think it’s shepherding us in a way ☺️☺️☺️☺️☺️ When we reached the ranger, it was about 1h into the climb. You can stop here to use the restroom. There was a small pavilion to rest and shelter in case of rain. Later I discovered on google reviews itself that the ranger station was for you to write your names in case you disappear. 😂 there really wasn’t much instructions going around and we pretty much fumbled and stumbled everywhere. Anyway, the peak was closed, so we only had the choice to go to Radar station. The view there was spending, very windy, and there were 1 or 2 horses there together with captive eagles and hawks. To take a picture with them was 50K vnd, but we did not. We left after about 30mins there, and begin our descent via the official paved path (too dangerous for the unofficial 😂) besides, it has started to drizzle abit. We left Radar station at 1pm, and reached the ticketing area at 2pm. Though not as stunning as the unofficial path, we still see some wildlife along the way like the horses and this really huge jungle cockroach. The descent was tough on our mid-30s knees 😂😂😂 so of course after that we had to go for a foot massage.

reviews.author: Vince
reviews.text: 8 miles roughly 5 hours. Took the longer route up because we couldn’t find the start which is actually right before the gate where you pay ticket fee. The last 50m on top of the mountain is right under the sun, but overall it was under the shade. Nice and breezy. Careful with pine trees as it can be slippery. Also found a dog at the top of the mountain. He guided us to the top. We also brought our dog and wasn’t sure if he was friendly. But he was harmless!

reviews.author: BCL Chanapai
reviews.text: The trekking path was enjoyable to walk. It gets steep and slippery once I get near to the top. The view is beautiful.

reviews.author: Clark NpL
reviews.text: great view, a lot of cars moving around so be careful

reviews.author: Oreo_cat:3
reviews.text: breathtaking view but most of the stuff there are statues and figures for check in purpose only. cannot use your own car and have to rent service car to take you up to the spot if you dont want a long uphill walk

reviews.author: Fiona Vo
reviews.text: Lang Biang Peak Trail – March Adventure • Weather in March? Absolutely perfect! Cool, no rain, gentle sunshine, dry trails—basically, Mother Nature is in a good mood. Plus, no annoying bugs leaving mystery red bites. • Greenery level: MAXIMUM GREEN. Probably the spring effect. Feels like hiking inside a fresh salad. • Snakes? Oh yeah! Met two friendly ones—one earthy brown and another rare, almost-extinct light red snake (if you see it, be a good human: don’t catch, don’t harm, just admire). Also, the local skinks here? Super long and skinny—fashionable little lizards! • Food & Water: Halfway up, there’s a ranger station where they log your info before you switch from hill trekking to mountain trail. Good news: you can buy water and biscuits here. Also, a much-needed break spot. Bonus: there’s a toilet. • At the peak: No cars, no cable cars, no restaurants, no cafés—just you, nature, and your thirst if you forget water. Plan accordingly! • Pro tip: Wear a hat. Stay hydrated. Have fun!

reviews.author: Oskar Collard
reviews.text: I appreciate the amazing views and infrastructure. As others have pointed out you must pay to enter the mountain which I haven't experienced in other countries. I feel like everything they add is to the detriment of hikers trying to enjoy the natural beauty of the area, you start the hike walking on the road as most tourists ride instead and there was no direct path from radar to the peak (unless I missed it but the map provided was not detailed). The path is natural most of the time but it could be modified to improve stability at some points. It's not their fault but people have littered everywhere and fires have destroyed some areas which is quite sad. I'm sure if I come back in the future this will be improved and I will give it 5 stars.

reviews.author: Katja Seeland
reviews.text: CANNOT recommend Langbiang Mt. peak unless you want to witness - local environmental destruction/ forest depletion - uncountable numbers of greenhouses - eliminition of natural habitat for Dalat province´s wildlife - SICK HORSES forced to do horseback riding for tourists although they are so unhealthy that they need to lay on the ground while their owners don´t care. It broke my heart (--> see fotos)!! - security staff scaring wild horses away from garden/restaurant ground using a SLINGSHOT to attack the horse (see my fotos)!! - raptors/birds being kept on leashes for fotos... - painted horses that look like zebras...??? What the hell... Too me, Langbiang Mt was just another example of consequences of the uncontrolled spread of humanity, the concurrent exploitation of nature and shocking animal conditions. I was disgusted by what I saw there. Not a place I would return to.

reviews.author: Vuong Nguyen An
reviews.text: Should spend time for trekking if you want to do excercise and find the new exciting things.

reviews.author: Hector Ritchie
reviews.text: Firstly had to pay an entrance fee for a walk????? We told the ticket desk we wanted to hike and we were simply pointed back behind the main entrance and not given any map or direction for this route. We started walking for at least 30 minutes and didn’t think it was the right way. I searched up a route online and there was a giant sign with skull and cross bones saying don’t go this way. So we turned round and walked all the way back to the ticket desk, we asked staff if we were going the right way and they said yes, just stay on that road and no turning off needed. We tried again and walked a slightly different route but only to be barked at by lots of dogs and followed. It made us feel quite uncomfortable, that and the death signs we decided just to abandon the hike. I really don’t recommend.

reviews.author: Nguyen Duc Minh
reviews.text: A popular sight-seeing for tourists and favorite hiking spot with challenging path leading to its peak

reviews.author: Tom Pham
reviews.text: I liked the view. Fun hike to the top and rewarding view. Picture is from a spot near the parking lot.

reviews.author: Norbi
reviews.text: The climb was alright, a bit touristy with all the tourists SUVs and the drivers are not nice, they drive as close to the pedestrians as they can, even though I think they are paid daily so no need to race... I left my raincoat for 10 mins on a bench while looking around, and another tourist stole it. We checked the security camera and asked every shop if he has returned it as a found item, but he hasn’t, so there was nothing I could do. Not a great memory, but of course that part is not the park’s fault, just keep your belongings close to you at all times☺️☺️

reviews.author: Philipp Kopper
reviews.text: Most pictures do NOT depict lang bian mountain but radar mountain. Lang bian mountain is gorgeous while radar is very bizarre and not recommended. You don’t need a ticket for lang bian but I would suggest you get one. It’s cheap (40k) and the park security personnel is rude and may obstruct your way if you want to pass through the easy way up. (There are two). All in all there are confusing information on the internet about the hike up. Also there are a few false closure signs. So better ask around before the hike.

reviews.author: Ryszard C
reviews.text: Don't support nature destruction. Nasty place.

reviews.author: Jay Nahata
reviews.text: Not worth the price and too crowded always, and only option is to by the jeep they provide

reviews.author: Jo Lim
reviews.text: View is nice but climb is tedious. Have to be prepared and fully equipped. Various parts are slippery

reviews.author: Luke Matthews
reviews.text: They corner you for no reason into paying for ridiculous things. I really want Vietnam to hire me to coordinate their tourism industry because whoever is doing it now is failing miserably.

reviews.author: Ben Leong
reviews.text: Lang Biang Mountain is a great spot to visit for beautiful panoramic views over Da Lat. The scenery from the top is stunning and well worth the trip up. You can choose between a private or public shuttle to reach the summit, both of which are convenient options. It’s perfect for a short visit of about one to two hours.

reviews.author: Jaws Lee
reviews.text: Becareful of bee like flies that will follow you and bite. Many people did camp fire, not allowed. And left rubbish around, including broken glasses. Initial part was easy, pine tree forest, fresh cool air with scent of pine tree. 260m to peak was most challenging, steep and slippery. Prepare to fall & get dirty. Many people left their rubbish on the peak.

reviews.author: tien tang
reviews.text: Đi xe jeep lên đỉnh núi đáng trải nghiệm, cảnh hoàng hôn đẹp, giá vé có thể chấp nhận, nếu đến nên trải nghiệm.
